children.heading: Border personnel requirements
children.label: 1 Border personnel requirements
children.text: A description of additional personnel required at border entry points, including a list of additional customs and agricultural inspectors.

children.heading: Agency staffing requirements
children.label: 2 Agency staffing requirements
children.text: A description of additional personnel required by Federal agencies responsible for monitoring and implementing the trade agreement, including personnel required by the Office of the United States Trade Representative, the Department of Commerce, the Department of Agriculture (including additional personnel required to implement sanitary and phytosanitary measures in order to obtain market access for United States exports), the Department of the Treasury, and such other agencies as may be necessary.

children.heading: Customs infrastructure requirements
children.label: 3 Customs infrastructure requirements
children.text: A description of the additional equipment and facilities needed by the United States Customs Service.

children.heading: Impact on State and local governments
children.label: 4 Impact on State and local governments
children.text: A description of the impact the trade agreement will have on State and local governments as a result of increases in trade.

children.heading: Cost analysis
children.label: 5 Cost analysis
children.text: An analysis of the costs associated with each of the items listed in paragraphs (1) through (4).

children.heading: Budget submission
children.label: b Budget submission
children.text: The President shall include a request for the resources necessary to support the plan described in subsection (a) in the first budget that the President submits to the Congress after the submission of the plan.
